name: create_draft
trigger_description:
Use this tool when:
The user asks to start, create, or save a draft email (new email or reply).
The user asks to write an email that may be sent now or later.
The user asks to send an email and no draft has been created or explicitly referenced earlier in the current conversational context (i.e., a draft that has been created, retrieved, or explicitly mentioned in the conversation).

Reply-to-Thread Rules
For reply and reply-all, infer recipients strictly from the original message or thread context.
When replying to a thread, omit the To field and rely on thread metadata for recipient resolution.
For reply-all, preserve the original Cc and Bcc recipients as present in the source message.
For replies, retrieve all required fields using the get_message tool.
Subject, Cc, and Bcc must exactly match the original email.
For reply drafts, threadId, inReplyTo, and references are mandatory.
The subject must remain unchanged from the original email.
The reply must maintain proper context from the original message.

Do not use this tool to add attachments (use add_attachments after creating the draft). If the user wants attachments, create the draft first, then call add_attachments.
